Architecture design: A high-level overview of the product's architecture, including the components, modules, and their interactions. 4. nodes.title: [2023-05-30 04:20] Rugged coastline of Shetland impacts local fishing industry
nodes.description: The rugged coastline of Shetland, marked by cliffs, rocky outcroppings, and shallow waters, makes it difficult for large boats to navigate close to shore, necessitating the use of smaller boats and fishing in shallow waters.

nodes.description: Rough sea conditions during winter months can prevent boats from going out to sea, posing challenges for fishermen in Shetland.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 04:20] Shetland fishing industry adapts to coastal conditions with new technologies
nodes.description: The fishing industry in Shetland has developed new technologies and fishing techniques to adapt to challenging coastal conditions, such as using creels instead of traditional fishing lines to catch shellfish.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 04:20] Shetland fishing industry provides employment and seafood
nodes.description: Despite challenges, the fishing industry continues to be an important part of Shetland's economy, providing employment for local people and producing high-quality seafood.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 04:20] Global warming impacts Shetland fishing industry
nodes.description: Global warming has significantly impacted the fishing industry in Shetland, affecting migration patterns and behavior of fish species like cod and haddock.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 04:20] Fish species in Shetland are moving due to global warming
nodes.description: As a result of global warming, some fish species have moved further north, while others have decreased in numbers or disappeared altogether, such as the decline of cod and the increase of mackerel.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 04:20] Shetland fishermen adapt to new conditions by targeting different species
nodes.description: Fishermen in Shetland have started to target different species of fish, such as crab and lobster, which have become more abundant due to changes in the marine environment.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 04:20] Shetland fishing industry adopts sustainable practices
nodes.description: The fishing industry in Shetland has been adopting various sustainable fishing practices, including selective fishing, use of creels, quota management, gear modification, and certification.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 04:20] Shetland fishing industry faces negative effects on marine ecosystem
nodes.description: The fishing industry in Shetland has negative effects on the marine ecosystem, including overfishing and damage to seafloor habitats from certain fishing gear.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 04:20] Shetland has potential for alternative industries to fishing
nodes.description: Shetland has potential for alternative industries such as renewable energy, tourism, aquaculture, and creative industries, leveraging its natural resources and culture.
